reviews.review_msg: Naoko is friendly & clearly passionate about teaching, but she is quite strict & inflexible, which is especially frustrating due to her policies. If you ever miss 5 classes, you can't take the exams. She rounds down decimals for final grades. She also puts a lot of focus on presentation projects that mostly rely on following scripts. Fun lectures.

She is often disrespectful towards students. She makes fun of them then treats it like a joke, unfunny jokes. Classes always run late, which is inconsiderate for students who have to walk across the campus to other classes. The listening quizzes were unfairly difficult, the class average was around 70% because it included upper level materials.

The class can be a bit intimidating, but Naoko makes it approachable and fun. You never feel stupid, despite her offhanded remarks. Funny, approachable, and a great teacher, Naoko is among one of the best profs at SFU.

I would take her class again if you are planning to continue taking Japanese class in SFU. Although she might be a bit difficult, if you are passionate about learning Japanese language she will guide you well.

Just a really sweet lady who rejuvenated my interest in learning Japanese. Shoe focuses on making the class fun, and tries to teach you not just the language, but also cultural elements as well. I learned not just Japanese, but about Japan as well. Only complaint is that she has to cancel/move move class online a lot.
Furukawa was a great prof who explained grammar and language concepts well. I'm very glad I decided to take her class!
